value.value: A human individual whose biographical data is being recorded.
A significant occurrence in a person's life, situated in time and optionally in space.
A span of time with a start and/or end date.
A structured group of people with a common purpose.
A recorded source of biographical information.
A function or position held by a person within a context.
A reified connection between two persons, with temporal extent and type.
A structured representation of a person's name, which may change over time.
An honorific or noble title (e.g., Duke, Sir, Dr.).
A textual approximation when exact dates are unknown (e.g., 'circa 1450', 'early 16th century').
Classification such as natural, accident, homicide, suicide, execution.
An archival reference or citation string.
An identifier in an external system (e.g., Wikidata QID, VIAF, ISNI).
A reified residence tying a person to a place during a time period.
A reified education period tying a person to an institution, degree, and time span.
A distinction, honor, medal, or prize received by a person.
A named period of history (e.g., Renaissance, Victorian Era).
A reified record of a person's financial status at a point in time.
The heraldic description of a coat of arms.
A lineage of rulers or a prominent family line.
Metadata about the source and confidence of a biographical assertion.
How certain the assertion is: certain, probable, possible, speculative.
A recorded description of a person's physical appearance at a point in time.
A reified record of a person's ownership of land or property.
A war, rebellion, or large-scale armed conflict.
A person who has at least one child.
A person who participated in at least one military event.
A person who has received at least one award.
A general relationship between a person and an organization.
Any part of a structured name.
A general super-property for any person-to-event link.
Links any reified assertion or entity to its provenance metadata.
Description of the legal sentence imposed (e.g., '10 years hard labor', 'death by hanging').
Links a career or education event to the organization where it occurred.
The first spouse in a marriage event.
The second spouse in a marriage event.
A reified participation linking a person to an event with a specific role (e.g., witness, officiant, judge, defendant).
The role of the participant (e.g., witness, officiant, judge, defendant, plaintiff).
Links a document to the time period it covers or was created in.
The time period during which this name was in use.
Links a person's nationality to the time period it was held. Modeled via a reified Nationality class.
A reified nationality tying a person to a country during a time period.
Links a person to a creative work (painting, photograph, sculpture) that depicts them.
Links a person to a reified Nationality record with temporal extent.
A person who participated in at least one graduation.
A person who has been imprisoned.
Links an award event to the award that was conferred.
Links a publication event to the creative work that was published.
The child being adopted in an adoption event.
The adoptive parent(s) in an adoption event.
The language in which a creative work was composed.
The name of the degree conferred (e.g., Bachelor of Arts, Doctor of Medicine).
